title: Dynamic tiering of datacenter power for workloads
abstract: Dynamic tiering of datacenter power for workloads is disclosed. A power capacity, including redundant power capacity and granular power capacity values within a datacenter, is determined. An outage time duration requirement for the power capacity that was determined is evaluated, where the outage time duration requirement is a number of minutes. A hold time duration requirement for the power capacity is evaluated, where the hold time duration is a number of minutes. A number of allowable occurrences of power outage for the power capacity is evaluated. A power requirement metric, based on the outage time duration requirement, the hold time duration requirement, and the number of occurrences, is calculated. A power topology within the datacenter is modified based on the power requirement metric. The modifying provides dynamic power tiering within the datacenter. The dynamic tiering includes a variable service level agreement for power within the datacenter.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A computer-implemented method for power management comprising: determining a power capacity within a datacenter; evaluating an outage time duration requirement for the power capacity that was determined; evaluating a hold time duration requirement for the power capacity; evaluating a number of occurrences of power outage allowed for the power capacity; calculating a power requirement metric based on the outage time duration requirement, the hold time duration requirement, and the number of occurrences; and modifying a power topology within the datacenter based on the power requirement metric. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein the modifying provides for a dynamic tiering of power within the datacenter. 3. The method of claim 2 wherein the dynamic tiering includes a variable service level agreement for power within the datacenter. 4. The method of claim 3 wherein the variable service level agreement enables 1N redundancy to 2N redundancy. 5. The method of claim 4 wherein the enabling provides incremental redundancy between 1N redundancy and 2N redundancy. 6. The method of claim 1 wherein the power capacity provides for dedicated slices of power availability during prescribed times. 7. The method of claim 1 wherein the power capacity provides for modifying a workload operation after a specified time period. 8 The method of claim 7 wherein the workload is a virtual machine. 9. The method of claim 1 wherein the power capacity provides for on-demand power capacity under specific conditions. 10. The method of claim 1 wherein the modifying enables support for varied application usage within the datacenter. 11. The method of claim 10 wherein the varied application usage is enabled at a service level agreement per application level. 12. The method of claim 1 wherein the modifying allows for modifying a virtual machine operation after a specified time period. 13. The method of claim 12 wherein the modifying includes moving, rescheduling, or powering off the virtual machine. 14. The method of claim 1 wherein the power capacity includes a redundant power capacity value. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the power capacity includes a set of granular power capacity values. 16. The method of claim 1 wherein the outage time duration requirement is represented as a number of minutes per year. 17. The method of claim 1 wherein the hold time duration requirement describes a number of minutes where a current power capacity is maintained. 18. The method of claim 1 wherein the number of occurrences is an integer number of outages allowed per year. 19. The method of claim 1 wherein the power topology includes a plurality of power sources. 20. The method of claim 1 further comprising determining availability factors for datacenter loads. 21. The method of claim 1 further comprising restructuring the power topology based on a variable SLA. 22. The method of claim 1 wherein the modifying includes reliability changes within a variable SLA. 23. The method of claim 1 further comprising determining a second power capacity within the datacenter, wherein the second power capacity enables calculating an additional power requirement metric. 24. The method of claim 23 wherein the additional power requirement metric includes an additional outage time duration requirement. 25. The method of claim 23 wherein the additional power requirement metric includes an additional hold time duration requirement. 26. The method of claim 23 wherein the determining a second power capacity includes a subset of the power capacity within the datacenter. 27. The method of claim 23 wherein the determining a second power capacity includes a portion of the power capacity within the datacenter not included in the determining of the power capacity. 28. The method of claim 1 wherein the determining includes distribution capacity within the datacenter. 29. The method of claim 1 wherein the determining includes rack-level granularity. 30. The method of claim 1 wherein the calculating includes peak shaving. 31. A computer program product embodied in a computer readable medium for power management, the computer program product comprising code which causes one or more processors to perform operations of: determining a power capacity within a datacenter; evaluating an outage time duration requirement for the power capacity that was determined; evaluating a hold time duration requirement for the power capacity; evaluating a number of occurrences of power outage allowed for the power capacity; calculating a power requirement metric based on the outage time duration requirement, the hold time duration requirement, and the number of occurrences; and modifying a power topology within the datacenter based on the power requirement metric. 32. The computer program product of claim 31 wherein the modifying provides for a dynamic tiering of power within the datacenter. 33. The computer program product of claim 32 wherein the dynamic tiering includes a variable service level agreement for power within the datacenter. 34. The computer program product of claim 33 wherein the variable service level agreement enables 1N redundancy to 2N redundancy. 35. The computer program product of claim 31 further comprising code for determining a second power capacity within the datacenter, wherein the second power capacity enables calculating an additional power requirement metric. 36. The computer program product of claim 35 wherein the additional power requirement metric includes an additional outage time duration requirement. 37. The computer program product of claim 35 wherein the additional power requirement metric includes an additional hold time duration requirement. 38. The computer program product of claim 35 wherein the determining a second power capacity includes a subset of the power capacity within the datacenter. 39. The computer program product of claim 31 wherein the determining includes distribution capacity within the datacenter. 40. A computer system for power management comprising: a memory which stores instructions; one or more processors coupled to the memory wherein the one or more processors, when executing the instructions which are stored, are configured to: determine a power capacity within a datacenter; evaluate an outage time duration requirement for the power capacity that was determined; evaluate a hold time duration requirement for the power capacity; evaluate a number of occurrences of power outage allowed for the power capacity; calculate a power requirement metric based on the outage time duration requirement, the hold time duration requirement, and the number of occurrences; and modify a power topology within the datacenter based on the power requirement metric. 41. The computer system of claim 40 wherein the modifying provides for a dynamic tiering of power within the datacenter. 42. The computer system of claim 41 wherein the dynamic tiering includes a variable service level agreement for power within the datacenter. 43. The computer system of claim 42 wherein the variable service level agreement enables 1N redundancy to 2N redundancy. 44. The computer system of claim 40 further configured to determine a second power capacity within the datacenter, wherein the second power capacity enables calculating an additional power requirement metric. 45. The computer system of claim 44 wherein the additional power requirement metric includes an additional outage time duration requirement. 46. The computer system of claim 44 wherein the additional power requirement metric includes an additional hold time duration requirement. 47. The computer system of claim 44 wherein the determining a second power capacity includes a subset of the power capacity within the datacenter. 48. The computer system of claim 40 wherein the determining includes distribution capacity within the datacenter.
